---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code.

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Ctrl+Shift+Enter*.



```{r}
# clearing the workspace
rm(list=ls(all=TRUE))
```

```{r}

view_flag<-TRUE
start_date <- "2014-08-01"
end_date <- "2019-12-01"

```

```{r}
# Loading the libraries that may prove useful
library("readxl")
library("stringi")
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(ggplot2)
library(skimr)

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(sqldf)
library(kableExtra)
library(plm)
library(lmtest)


# library(xlsx)
library(reshape2)
library(tibble)
library(panelr)
library(multiway)
library(stringr)
library(zoo)
library(arsenal)
library("data.table")
library(readr)
library(sqldf)
library(stringr)
library(fuzzyjoin)
library(lubridate)
library(MatchIt)
library(janitor)
library(forcats)
library(officer)
library(magrittr)
library(gridExtra)
library(magick)
library(grid)

library(panelView)
library(MatchIt)
library(cobalt)
library(DescTools)
library(car)
library(writexl)
library(fixest)
library(modelsummary)


```


```{r setup}
# This sets the working directory for the subsequent chunks but not the current chunk. Its a bit weird
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = normalizePath("C:/Users/rahul/OneDrive - BITSoM/Desktop/Phd/ABFRL/R_directory"))

out_folder <- "ppt_pics2"
if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)
```


```{r}

store_list <- read_excel("C:/Users/rahul/OneDrive - BITSoM/Desktop/PhD/ABFRL/R_directory/Data/Swayam_Sharma_Store Master - PT.xlsx")

#clean names
store_list <- store_list %>%
  clean_names()


n_distinct(store_list$site_code)


```

```{r}

df_NOB <- read_excel("C:/Users/rahul/OneDrive - BITSoM/Desktop/PhD/ABFRL/R_directory/Data/Swayam_Sharma_Footfall & NOB.xlsx", 
    sheet = "DATA")
df_NOB <- df_NOB %>%  clean_names()


df_NOB <- merge(df_NOB, store_list[,c("site_code","state","type","carpet_area_sq_ft","store_classification")], by="site_code", all.x=TRUE)

```


```{r}
df_sales <- read_excel("C:/Users/rahul/OneDrive - BITSoM/Desktop/PhD/ABFRL/R_directory/Data/Swayam_Sharma_Sales & Inven.xlsx", 
    sheet = "SalesData")

df_sales <- df_sales %>% clean_names()


colnames(df_sales)
```


```{r}
df_inventory <- read_excel("C:/Users/rahul/OneDrive - BITSoM/Desktop/PhD/ABFRL/R_directory/Data/Swayam_Sharma_Sales & Inven.xlsx", 
    sheet = "InvenData")

df_inventory <- df_inventory %>% clean_names()
colnames(df_inventory)

```

```{r}
df_shirts <- read.csv("C:/Users/rahul/OneDrive - BITSoM/Desktop/PhD/ABFRL/R_directory/Data/Swayam_Sharma_SHIRTS - Sales Data.csv")
colnames(df_shirts)




df_shirts2 <- read.csv("C:/Users/rahul/OneDrive - BITSoM/Desktop/PhD/ABFRL/R_directory/Data/Swayam_Sharma_SHIRTS - Stock Data.csv")
colnames(df_shirts2)
# df_shirts2 <- df_shirts2 %>% clean_names()
```


```{r}
# ── Preprocess df_shirts (clean names, rename, create wave index) ──────────────
df_shirts <- df_shirts %>% clean_names()
df_shirts <- df_shirts %>%
  rename(brand = new_brand_description, sku = option_code, store = site_code)

df_shirts$fy_year <- str_replace(df_shirts$fy_year, "-2025", "")
df_shirts$fy_year <- str_replace(df_shirts$fy_year, "-2026", "")

df_shirts <- df_shirts %>%
  filter(!is.na(fy_year), !is.na(week)) %>%
  mutate(
    week_num    = as.integer(str_remove(week, "^Week")),
    fy_year_num = as.numeric(as.character(fy_year))
  )

max_week <- max(df_shirts$week_num, na.rm = TRUE)
min_year <- min(df_shirts$fy_year_num, na.rm = TRUE)

df_shirts <- df_shirts %>%
  mutate(wave = (fy_year_num - min_year) * max_week + week_num)

# Store-week aggregates
df_shirts <- df_shirts %>%
  group_by(store, wave) %>%
  mutate(n_skus = n_distinct(sku), net_qty = sum(sls_qty), net_sales = sum(nsv)) %>%
  ungroup()

# Merge store metadata
df_shirts <- merge(
  df_shirts,
  store_list[, c("site_code", "state", "type", "carpet_area_sq_ft", "store_classification")],
  by.x = "store", by.y = "site_code", all.x = TRUE
)
```


```{r}
# ── Store-level weekly sales: aggregate trend ──────────────────────────────────
df_store_week <- df_shirts %>%
  select(store, wave, fy_year_num, week_num, net_sales) %>%
  distinct()

df_agg_weekly <- df_store_week %>%
  group_by(wave, fy_year_num, week_num) %>%
  summarise(
    median_sales = median(net_sales, na.rm = TRUE),
    q25      = quantile(net_sales, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    q75      = quantile(net_sales, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_stores = n_distinct(store),
    .groups  = "drop"
  )

p_agg <- ggplot(df_agg_weekly, aes(x = wave)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = q25, ymax = q75), fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_line(aes(y = median_sales), color = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title    = "Weekly Sales Distribution Across Stores",
    subtitle = "Line = median; band = IQR (25th–75th percentile)",
    x = "Wave (week index)", y = "Net Sales"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

p_agg
```


```{r}
# ── Store-level heatmap: sales relative to each store's median ─────────────────
df_store_week_norm <- df_store_week %>%
  group_by(store) %>%
  mutate(
    store_median = median(net_sales, na.rm = TRUE),
    sales_idx    = net_sales / store_median   # 1 = store's typical week
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

p_heatmap <- ggplot(
  df_store_week_norm,
  aes(x = wave, y = reorder(store, store_median), fill = sales_idx)
) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red",
    midpoint = 1, limits = c(0.2, 2), oob = scales::squish,
    name = "Rel. Sales\n(store median = 1)"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Store-Level Sales Relative to Each Store's Median",
    x = "Wave (week index)", y = "Store (ordered by median sales)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank())

p_heatmap
```


```{r}
# ── 2-week rolling stability: find the most stable window ─────────────────────
all_waves <- sort(unique(df_store_week$wave))

window_list <- lapply(seq_len(length(all_waves) - 1), function(i) {
  w1 <- all_waves[i]
  w2 <- all_waves[i + 1]

  # Skip if weeks are not truly adjacent (gap > 2 means missing data)
  if (w2 - w1 > 2) return(NULL)

  s1 <- df_store_week %>% filter(wave == w1) %>% transmute(store, sales_w1 = net_sales)
  s2 <- df_store_week %>% filter(wave == w2) %>% transmute(store, sales_w2 = net_sales)

  paired <- inner_join(s1, s2, by = "store") %>%
    filter(sales_w1 > 0, sales_w2 > 0) %>%
    mutate(
      midpoint    = (sales_w1 + sales_w2) / 2,
      abs_pct_chg = abs(sales_w2 - sales_w1) / midpoint
    )

  if (nrow(paired) < 5) return(NULL)

  data.frame(
    wave1              = w1,
    wave2              = w2,
    n_stores           = nrow(paired),
    mean_abs_pct_chg   = mean(paired$abs_pct_chg,   na.rm = TRUE),
    median_abs_pct_chg = median(paired$abs_pct_chg, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_stores_lt20    = mean(paired$abs_pct_chg < 0.20, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_stores_lt10    = mean(paired$abs_pct_chg < 0.10, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
})

window_stability <- Filter(Negate(is.null), window_list) %>% bind_rows()

# Attach readable labels
wave_labels <- df_store_week %>%
  select(wave, fy_year_num, week_num) %>%
  distinct()

window_stability <- window_stability %>%
  left_join(wave_labels %>% rename(wave1 = wave, fy1 = fy_year_num, wk1 = week_num), by = "wave1") %>%
  left_join(wave_labels %>% rename(wave2 = wave, fy2 = fy_year_num, wk2 = week_num), by = "wave2") %>%
  mutate(window_label = paste0("FY", fy1, " W", wk1, "–W", wk2))

best_windows <- window_stability %>% arrange(mean_abs_pct_chg) %>% head(10)

cat("Top 10 most stable 2-week windows (lowest mean |% change| across stores):\n")
print(
  best_windows %>%
    transmute(
      Window              = window_label,
      N_Stores            = n_stores,
      Mean_AbsPctChg_pct  = round(mean_abs_pct_chg * 100, 1),
      Stores_lt20pct      = paste0(round(pct_stores_lt20 * 100, 0), "%"),
      Stores_lt10pct      = paste0(round(pct_stores_lt10 * 100, 0), "%")
    ),
  row.names = FALSE
)
```


```{r}
# ── Visualise stability over time + highlight best windows ────────────────────
top3 <- best_windows %>% head(3)

p_stability <- ggplot(window_stability, aes(x = wave1, y = mean_abs_pct_chg)) +
  geom_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_point(data = top3,
             aes(x = wave1, y = mean_abs_pct_chg),
             color = "green4", size = 4, shape = 17) +
  geom_text(data = top3,
            aes(x = wave1, y = mean_abs_pct_chg, label = window_label),
            color = "green4", size = 2.8, vjust = -0.8, hjust = 0.5) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    title    = "2-Week Sales Stability Across Stores",
    subtitle = "Lower = more stable | Green triangles = top 3 most stable windows",
    x        = "Wave (start of 2-week window)",
    y        = "Mean |% Change| in Store Sales"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

p_stability

# Summary table
top_windows_tbl <- best_windows %>%
  head(5) %>%
  transmute(
    Window               = window_label,
    `N Stores`           = n_stores,
    `Mean |% Change|`    = paste0(round(mean_abs_pct_chg * 100, 1), "%"),
    `Stores < 20% chg`   = paste0(round(pct_stores_lt20 * 100, 0), "%"),
    `Stores < 10% chg`   = paste0(round(pct_stores_lt10 * 100, 0), "%")
  )

kable(top_windows_tbl, format = "html",
      caption = "Top 5 Most Stable 2-Week Sales Windows") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"))
```
